questions.question_text: How many total hours of pre-license education must a Tennessee affiliate broker candidate complete?
questions.explanation: Tennessee requires 90 total hours: 60 hours of Real Estate Principles (before the exam) plus a 30-hour Course for New Affiliates (before license issuance).

questions.question_text: A candidate has completed 60 hours of Real Estate Principles. What must happen before the license is issued?
questions.correct_answer: Complete the 30-hour Course for New Affiliates
distractors: Work 6 months under a principal broker
questions.explanation: After passing the exam (which requires only the 60-hour course), the candidate must complete the 30-hour Course for New Affiliates before the actual license can be issued.

questions.question_text: What is the minimum age to obtain a Tennessee affiliate broker license?
questions.explanation: Tennessee requires affiliate broker candidates to be at least 18 years old. This is the same minimum age as most other states.

questions.question_text: When are fingerprints required for a Tennessee real estate license?
questions.correct_answer: At the time of initial license application
distractors: Before sitting for the PSI exam
questions.explanation: Fingerprints are required for all initial license applications in Tennessee but are not required for the exam itself. This is a background check requirement for initial licensure.

questions.question_text: A candidate passes the 60-hour Real Estate Principles course. Which of the following is true about taking the PSI exam?
questions.correct_answer: The candidate may take the exam now
distractors: The 30-hour course must be done first
A principal broker must sponsor them first
They must wait 6 months after the course
questions.explanation: Only the 60-hour Real Estate Principles course is required before taking the PSI exam. The 30-hour Course for New Affiliates must be completed before the license is issued, not before the exam.

questions.question_text: After passing the PSI exam, how long does a candidate have to apply for a Tennessee license?
questions.explanation: Candidates must apply for their license within 1 year of passing the exam. After that, the exam results expire and the candidate must retake the exam.

questions.question_text: An affiliate broker changes their home address. What must they do?
questions.correct_answer: Notify TREC of the address change
distractors: Obtain a new E&O insurance policy
questions.explanation: Changes of name or address require notification to TREC. This is an administrative update, not a new application or exam requirement.

questions.question_text: Under whom must a Tennessee affiliate broker work?
distractors: Any licensed broker in the state
questions.explanation: Tennessee affiliate brokers must work under a principal broker, the supervising broker of a firm. The principal broker oversees the affiliate's activities and ensures compliance with all laws.

questions.question_text: A brokerage firm wants to operate in Tennessee. What must the firm do regarding licensing?
questions.correct_answer: Register with TREC as a licensed firm
distractors: Only the principal broker needs a license
File with the Secretary of State only
Obtain a general business license only
questions.explanation: In Tennessee, the firm itself must be licensed and registered with TREC, in addition to individual licensees. TREC requires firm licensing to maintain oversight of brokerage operations.

questions.question_text: Where must a Tennessee real estate license be displayed?
questions.explanation: Tennessee requires that licenses be displayed at the principal office of the firm. This allows clients and TREC inspectors to verify active licensure.

questions.question_text: How many hours of continuing education must a Tennessee licensee complete per renewal cycle?
questions.explanation: Tennessee requires 16 hours of continuing education per renewal cycle for all licensees, after any post-licensing requirements have been completed.

questions.question_text: A broker obtained their license in 2020. How many hours of post-license education are required and by when?
distractors: 16 hours within the first renewal cycle
questions.explanation: Brokers licensed after January 1, 2005 must complete 120 additional hours of education within 3 years of initial broker license issuance, before regular CE requirements apply.

questions.question_text: A principal broker who was previously exempt from CE due to grandfathering now supervises affiliate brokers. What CE is required?
distractors: No CE is required due to grandfathering
questions.explanation: TREC removed the grandfathering exemption. All principal brokers supervising affiliate brokers must now complete 16 hours of CE each renewal cycle, regardless of prior exempt status.

questions.question_text: An affiliate broker is approaching their first license renewal. What must they complete?
questions.explanation: For renewal, affiliate brokers must complete 16 hours of CE. The 30-hour Course for New Affiliates is a one-time pre-license requirement, and the 120-hour post-license applies to brokers only.

questions.question_text: A broker licensed in 2003 wants to know about post-license education requirements. Which applies?
questions.correct_answer: Post-license not required for pre-2005 brokers
questions.explanation: The 120-hour post-license requirement applies only to brokers licensed after January 1, 2005. Brokers licensed before that date are exempt from this particular requirement.
